Untitled Project
GHOST Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Requirements
Document
  • VIEWS
  • Document
  • Table
  • Traceability
  • Deep Traceability

    • GHOST Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Requirements

    UID: GHOST-MEMORY-MERGE-ACTIVEBRIEF-BLACKBOARD
    VERSION: 0.1

    1. 1. Purpose and Scope

    STATEMENT: This document defines the current Memory ActiveRetrievalBrief, deterministic memory reduction, query-time episodic compression, Memory Context synthesis, SuperPrompt handoff, and the explicit current boundary toward a future Blackboard capability.

    It owns current runtime transformation and continuity behavior after durable recording and candidate retrieval. It does not redefine durable MemoryRecord truth, vector ingestion, or document A3/A4 behavior.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
    1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    UID: GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-PARENT-CAPABILITY Parent Memory Capability
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ACTIVEBRIEF-SCOPE ActiveBrief Scope
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-COMPRESSION-SCOPE Runtime Compression Scope
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-SYNTHESIS-SCOPE Memory Synthesis Scope
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-DURABLE-OWNERSHIP No Durable Truth Ownership
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-DOCUMENT-EVIDENCE No Document Evidence Ownership
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-FINAL-ANSWER No Final User Answer
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-LOCATION-MAP Implementation Location Map
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-RECORDING-TIME Recording-Time Functional Group
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-RETRIEVAL-TIME Retrieval-Time Functional Group
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-SHARED-REDUCER Shared Reducer Boundary
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AGENT-STACK-USE Agent Stack Use
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-STATE-OWNERS State Ownership Map
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-UI-ALGORITHM No UI Algorithm Ownership
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT Reducer Input Contract
    • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-RAM RAM-Only Pending Topic State
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT Runtime Memory Compressor
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT Memory Context Synthesizer
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-PACK Pack Synthesis Assignment
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SP-TEXT SuperPrompt Memory Context Text
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SP-PACK SuperPrompt Raw Memory Pack
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-ACTIVEBRIEF SuperPrompt ActiveBrief Snapshot
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-RENDER Normal Memory Context Rendering
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-ACTIVEBRIEF-MODE ActiveBrief Projection Mode Dependency
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-PERMISSION Memory Context Permission Dependency
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-NO-DOC-MUTATION No Document Context Mutation
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-NO-STAGE-CLAIM No Independent Pipeline Stage Claim
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-NO-PERSIST No Runtime Context Persistence
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-RUNTIME Runtime Configuration Source
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-ACTIVE-LOAD Recording-Time ActiveBrief Configuration Load
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-ACTIVE-REDUCER Consumed ActiveBrief Reducer Settings
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-ACTIVE-GATE Consumed ActiveBrief Gate Settings
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-ACTIVE-LLM Consumed ActiveBrief LLM Settings
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-COMPRESS Consumed Compression Settings
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-SYNTH Consumed Synthesis Settings
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-AGENT-AUTHORITY Agent Definition Authority
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-NO-HIDDEN-OVERRIDE No Hidden Configuration Override
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-MIGRATION Operational Parameter Migration
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-VALIDATION Configuration Validation Requirement
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ERROR-DURABLE Transformation Failure Does Not Rewrite Truth
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ERROR-EMPTY Empty Runtime Result Is Explicit
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ERROR-ACTIVE ActiveBrief Error Visibility
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-LOG-PUBLIC Public Logging Boundary
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-LOG-DEVELOPER Developer Diagnostic Boundary
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-LOG-GATE Gate Log Summaries
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-DETERMINISTIC-TEST Deterministic Component Testability
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AGENT-TEST Agent Contract Testability
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-REPRODUCIBILITY Decision Reproducibility
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-OUTSIDE-KNOWLEDGE No Outside Knowledge in Memory Artifacts
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-SEMANTIC-GUARANTEE LLM Compression Nature
    • GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-NOT-IMPLEMENTED No Current Blackboard Subsystem
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-CODE-OWNERSHIP Memory Merge Code Ownership
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-SIBLING-BOUNDARY Sibling Ownership Boundary
    STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
    1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
    2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
    3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
    4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
    5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
    6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-PARENT-CAPABILITY
    1.2. Parent Memory Capability
    UID: GHOST-MEM-MERGE-PARENT-CAPABILITY
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    STATEMENT: This document shall refine the Memory Management capability defined by 50_memory_management.sdoc and shall preserve the authority boundaries defined by 51_memory_recording_and_files.sdoc and 52_memory_ingestion_and_retrieval.sdoc.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ACTIVEBRIEF-SCOPE
    1.3. ActiveBrief Scope
    UID: GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ACTIVEBRIEF-SCOPE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    STATEMENT: ActiveRetrievalBrief requirements in this document shall cover creation, update, propagation, contributor tracking, relevance gating, and pending-topic handling performed during memory recording.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-COMPRESSION-SCOPE
    1.4. Runtime Compression Scope
    UID: GHOST-MEM-MERGE-COMPRESSION-SCOPE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    STATEMENT: Runtime compression requirements in this document shall cover query reduction and query-anchored reduction of selected episodic Q/A candidates without modifying durable source records.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-SYNTHESIS-SCOPE
    1.5. Memory Synthesis Scope
    UID: GHOST-MEM-MERGE-SYNTHESIS-SCOPE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    STATEMENT: Memory synthesis requirements in this document shall cover evidence formatting, Agent Stack invocation, structured output parsing, diagnostics, and write-back of synthesized Memory Context.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-DURABLE-OWNERSHIP
    1.6. No Durable Truth Ownership
    UID: GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-DURABLE-OWNERSHIP
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    STATEMENT: MemorySentenceReducer, MemoryCompressor, MemoryMergeSynthesizer, and the relevance gate shall not own .ragmem, .ragmeta.json, SQLite, or memory-vector truth.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-DOCUMENT-EVIDENCE
    1.7. No Document Evidence Ownership
    UID: GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-DOCUMENT-EVIDENCE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    STATEMENT: This document shall not assign document retrieval, reranking, A3 usefulness selection, A4 condensation, or S_CTX_MD ownership to Memory Management.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-FINAL-ANSWER
    1.8. No Final User Answer
    UID: GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-FINAL-ANSWER
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    STATEMENT: ActiveBrief and Memory Context agents shall produce backend context artifacts and shall not answer the current user request.

    2. 2. Implementation Location Map and Functional Decomposition

    STATEMENT: The current implementation is distributed across recording-time continuity components, retrieval-time compression and synthesis components, agent definitions, and SuperPrompt projection.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-LOCATION-MAP
    2.1. Implementation Location Map
    UID: GHOST-MEM-MERGE-LOCATION-MAP
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    STATEMENT: The current implementation shall be traceable through these locations:
    - ragstream/memory/compression/memory_sentence_reducer.py: shared deterministic Q/A window reduction;
    - ragstream/memory/compression/memory_activebrief_relevance_gate.py: non-LLM ActiveBrief routing gate;
    - ragstream/memory/compression/memory_active_retrieval_brief.py: recording-time ActiveBrief orchestration;
    - ragstream/memory/compression/memory_compressor.py: query-time episodic compression;
    - ragstream/memory/memory_merge_synthesizer.py: runtime Memory Context synthesis;
    - data/agents/memory_activebrief_qa_summarizer/: ActiveBrief init/update agent definitions;
    - data/agents/memory_synthesizer/memory_synthesizer_001.json: Memory Context agent definition;
    - ragstream/memory/memory_manager.py: recording-time invocation and durable write authority;
    - ragstream/retrieval/retriever_mem.py: retrieval-time invocation and SuperPrompt write-back;
    - ragstream/orchestration/super_prompt.py and superprompt_projector.py: runtime fields and rendering.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-RECORDING-TIME
    2.2. Recording-Time Functional Group
    UID: GHOST-MEM-MERGE-RECORDING-TIME
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    STATEMENT: ActiveBrief reduction, relevance gating, init/update agent execution, pending-topic maintenance, and stable ActiveBrief assignment shall execute as part of MemoryRecord capture before the new stable body is appended.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-RETRIEVAL-TIME
    2.3. Retrieval-Time Functional Group
    UID: GHOST-MEM-MERGE-RETRIEVAL-TIME
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    STATEMENT: Query anchoring, episodic compression, evidence assembly, Memory Context synthesis, and MemoryContextPack update shall execute as runtime retrieval functions after memory candidates have been selected.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-SHARED-REDUCER
    2.4. Shared Reducer Boundary
    UID: GHOST-MEM-MERGE-SHARED-REDUCER
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    STATEMENT: MemorySentenceReducer shall be the shared deterministic reduction component for recording-time centroid reduction and retrieval-time external-query-anchor reduction.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AGENT-STACK-USE
    2.5. Agent Stack Use
    UID: GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AGENT-STACK-USE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    STATEMENT: ActiveBrief and Memory Context LLM calls shall resolve versioned AgentPrompt definitions through AgentFactory and shall invoke the provider through LLMClient as defined by 20_agent_stack.sdoc.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-STATE-OWNERS
    2.6. State Ownership Map
    UID: GHOST-MEM-MERGE-STATE-OWNERS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    STATEMENT: MemoryManager shall own pending-topic RAM state and durable ActiveBrief assignment; MemoryContextPack shall own run-local candidate and synthesis results; SuperPrompt shall own run-local final memory fields.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-UI-ALGORITHM
    2.7. No UI Algorithm Ownership
    UID: GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-UI-ALGORITHM
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    STATEMENT: Streamlit callbacks and views may trigger or display these functions but shall not own ActiveBrief routing, reduction, synthesis, or state-authority rules.

    3. 3. ActiveRetrievalBrief Recording Position and Authority

    STATEMENT: ActiveRetrievalBrief is a query-independent continuity artifact created or propagated when a new MemoryRecord is captured.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION
    3.1. ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-TITLE ActiveBrief Operational Title
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-RECORDING-POSITION ActiveBrief Before Durable Append
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-MANAGER-AUTHORITY MemoryManager Write Authority
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-STABLE-BODY ActiveBrief Stable Body Fields
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-QUERY-INDEPENDENCE Query Independence
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-NO-RECORD-REWRITE No Prior Record Rewrite
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CAPTURE-FAILURE ActiveBrief Failure Isolation
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-IMPORTED-BRIEF Imported Shared Brief Assignment
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-IMPORT-NO-AUTO Imported Records Without Supplied Brief
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-PREVIOUS-SCAN Newest-to-Oldest Previous Brief Scan
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CURRENT-METADATA Current Record Metadata Payload
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT ActiveBrief Relevance Gate
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT Initial Brief Route
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-GATE-DISABLED Gate Disabled Route
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-NORMAL-UPDATE Related ActiveBrief Update Route
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SAFE-PASS Safe-Pass Update Route
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-UPDATE-CONTRIBUTORS Update Contributor Merge
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-UPDATE-BUFFER Update Clears Pending Buffer
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-SHIFT Confirmed Topic-Shift Route
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SKIP Unrelated Q/A Skip Route
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-NO-FABRICATION No Fabricated ActiveBrief Fallback
    • GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-STABLE Contributor IDs as Stable Brief Trace
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-ID ActiveBrief Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be a compact, query-independent representation of the current working-conversation center for one memory history.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-TITLE
    3.2. ActiveBrief Operational Title
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-TITLE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    STATEMENT: Each non-empty ActiveRetrievalBrief produced by the current summarizer contract shall be accompanied by active_retrieval_brief_title as an operational backend title.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-RECORDING-POSITION
    3.3. ActiveBrief Before Durable Append
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-RECORDING-POSITION
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    STATEMENT: MemoryManager.capture_pair shall attempt to build the new record ActiveBrief before appending that MemoryRecord stable body to .ragmem.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-MANAGER-AUTHORITY
    3.4. MemoryManager Write Authority
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-MANAGER-AUTHORITY
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    STATEMENT: MemoryActiveRetrievalBriefBuilder shall return its result to MemoryManager and shall not write files, SQLite rows, or vectors directly.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-STABLE-BODY
    3.5. ActiveBrief Stable Body Fields
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-STABLE-BODY
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    STATEMENT: MemoryManager shall assign ActiveBrief title, body, and contributor IDs to the new MemoryRecord before persistence so those fields become part of the append-only .ragmem stable body.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-QUERY-INDEPENDENCE
    3.6. Query Independence
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-QUERY-INDEPENDENCE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    STATEMENT: Recording-time ActiveBrief generation shall use the current record, prior clean ActiveBrief, record metadata, and pending-topic state and shall not use the later user retrieval query or document evidence.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-NO-RECORD-REWRITE
    3.7. No Prior Record Rewrite
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-NO-RECORD-REWRITE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    STATEMENT: Creating the ActiveBrief for record K shall not rewrite the stable body of earlier MemoryRecords.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CAPTURE-FAILURE
    3.8. ActiveBrief Failure Isolation
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CAPTURE-FAILURE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    STATEMENT: If ActiveBrief generation raises an exception inside MemoryManager, MemoryManager shall log the developer failure when enabled and shall continue the durable MemoryRecord capture path without fabricating a brief.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-IMPORTED-BRIEF
    3.9. Imported Shared Brief Assignment
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-IMPORTED-BRIEF
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    STATEMENT: ChatGPT shared-link import may assign one user-supplied MemoryBrief title and body to every imported record; that assignment shall be distinguished from per-record ActiveBrief generation by the recording-time builder.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-IMPORT-NO-AUTO
    3.10. Imported Records Without Supplied Brief
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-IMPORT-NO-AUTO
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    STATEMENT: When a ChatGPT shared-link import contains no supplied MemoryBrief, the current import path shall persist imported records with empty ActiveBrief fields rather than silently running the normal per-record ActiveBrief builder.

    4. 4. MemorySentenceReducer Contract

    STATEMENT: MemorySentenceReducer performs deterministic sentence-window reduction while retaining vector evidence required by the ActiveBrief gate.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT
    4.1. Reducer Input Contract
    UID: GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-CONFIG Reducer Configuration Fields
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-SENTENCE-SPLIT Sentence Segmentation
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-WINDOWS Sentence Windows
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-ALWAYS-EMBED Always Vectorize Prepared Windows
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-CENTROID-MODE Centroid Reduction Mode
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-EXTERNAL-ANCHOR External Anchor Reduction Mode
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-BUDGET Q/A Budget Allocation
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-BELOW-BUDGET Below-Budget Preservation
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-SCORE Window Relevance Score
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-REDUNDANCY Window Redundancy Exclusion
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOKEN-FIT Window Token Fit
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOP-FALLBACK Top-Window Fallback
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-ORDER Original Sentence Order Restoration
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-VECTOR-OUTPUT Surviving Vector Output
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-FALLBACK-SLICE Bounded Text Slice Fallback
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOKEN-COUNT Token Estimation
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-COSINE-SAFETY Cosine Safety
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-NO-DURABLE-MUTATION Reducer Runtime Output Only
    STATEMENT: MemorySentenceReducer shall accept input_text and output_text and shall return ReducedQA containing reduced_question, reduced_answer, question_vectors, answer_vectors, and diagnostics.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-CONFIG
    4.2. Reducer Configuration Fields
    UID: GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-CONFIG
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT Reducer Input Contract
    STATEMENT: The reducer shall expose max_tokens_total, question_max_tokens, window_size_sentences, window_overlap_sentences, redundancy_threshold, and embedding_model as construction parameters.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-SENTENCE-SPLIT
    4.3. Sentence Segmentation
    UID: GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-SENTENCE-SPLIT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT Reducer Input Contract
    STATEMENT: The reducer shall split text using sentence-ending punctuation and line breaks and shall omit empty sentence units.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-WINDOWS
    4.4. Sentence Windows
    UID: GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-WINDOWS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT Reducer Input Contract
    STATEMENT: The reducer shall form ordered sentence windows with window size at least one, overlap clamped below window size, and positive step equal to window size minus overlap.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-ALWAYS-EMBED
    4.5. Always Vectorize Prepared Windows
    UID: GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-ALWAYS-EMBED
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT Reducer Input Contract
    STATEMENT: The reducer shall embed prepared question and answer windows even when the original side is already within its token budget so the resulting vectors remain available to the relevance gate.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-CENTROID-MODE
    4.6. Centroid Reduction Mode
    UID: GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-CENTROID-MODE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT Reducer Input Contract
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-NO-VECTORS No-Vector Centroid Result
    STATEMENT: reduce_with_centroid shall compute an anchor from the centroid of all compatible question and answer vectors and shall use that anchor for query-independent reduction.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-EXTERNAL-ANCHOR
    4.7. External Anchor Reduction Mode
    UID: GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-EXTERNAL-ANCHOR
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT Reducer Input Contract
    STATEMENT: reduce_with_anchor shall reduce the prepared Q/A against a caller-supplied anchor vector and shall identify the diagnostic mode as external_anchor.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-NO-VECTORS
    4.8. No-Vector Centroid Result
    UID: GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-NO-VECTORS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-CENTROID-MODE Centroid Reduction Mode
    STATEMENT: When centroid mode has no Q/A vectors, the reducer shall return empty reduced text and vectors with a no_vectors diagnostic rather than fabricate content.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-BUDGET
    4.9. Q/A Budget Allocation
    UID: GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-BUDGET
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT Reducer Input Contract
    STATEMENT: The reducer shall allocate the question share proportionally to original Q/A token estimates, cap it by question_max_tokens, provide a positive fallback for a non-empty question, and allocate the remaining total budget to the answer.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-BELOW-BUDGET
    4.10. Below-Budget Preservation
    UID: GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-BELOW-BUDGET
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT Reducer Input Contract
    STATEMENT: When one side is already within its allocated budget, the reducer shall preserve that side text and all of its prepared vectors.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-SCORE
    4.11. Window Relevance Score
    UID: GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-SCORE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT Reducer Input Contract
    STATEMENT: For a side requiring reduction, the reducer shall rank each compatible window by cosine similarity to the active anchor vector.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-REDUNDANCY
    4.12. Window Redundancy Exclusion
    UID: GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-REDUNDANCY
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT Reducer Input Contract
    STATEMENT: The reducer shall skip a candidate window when its cosine similarity to any already selected window is greater than or equal to redundancy_threshold.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOKEN-FIT
    4.13. Window Token Fit
    UID: GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOKEN-FIT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT Reducer Input Contract
    STATEMENT: The reducer shall skip a candidate window whose estimated tokens would cause selected window tokens to exceed the side budget.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOP-FALLBACK
    4.14. Top-Window Fallback
    UID: GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOP-FALLBACK
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT Reducer Input Contract
    STATEMENT: If no ranked window can be selected but scored windows exist, the reducer shall retain the highest-scoring window as the selection fallback.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-ORDER
    4.15. Original Sentence Order Restoration
    UID: GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-ORDER
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT Reducer Input Contract
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-REDUCER AC: Reducer Order and Budget
    STATEMENT: The reducer shall reconstruct reduced text from selected sentence indexes in their original source order rather than relevance-rank order.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-VECTOR-OUTPUT
    4.16. Surviving Vector Output
    UID: GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-VECTOR-OUTPUT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT Reducer Input Contract
    STATEMENT: When reduction occurs, returned question_vectors and answer_vectors shall correspond to the selected windows rather than all discarded windows.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-FALLBACK-SLICE
    4.17. Bounded Text Slice Fallback
    UID: GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-FALLBACK-SLICE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT Reducer Input Contract
    STATEMENT: When over-budget text cannot be reconstructed from valid windows, the reducer shall use the current bounded character-slice fallback and shall not create new semantic content.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOKEN-COUNT
    4.18. Token Estimation
    UID: GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOKEN-COUNT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT Reducer Input Contract
    STATEMENT: The reducer shall use cl100k_base through tiktoken when available and shall otherwise estimate tokens as the ceiling of character count divided by four.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-COSINE-SAFETY
    4.19. Cosine Safety
    UID: GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-COSINE-SAFETY
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT Reducer Input Contract
    STATEMENT: Cosine comparison shall return zero for empty vectors, dimensional mismatch, or zero vector norms.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-NO-DURABLE-MUTATION
    4.20. Reducer Runtime Output Only
    UID: GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-NO-DURABLE-MUTATION
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT Reducer Input Contract
    STATEMENT: Reducer output shall not modify the original MemoryRecord input_text or output_text.

    5. 5. ActiveBrief Previous-State Selection

    STATEMENT: The ActiveBrief builder selects one previous clean brief from the active in-memory history before deciding how the new record affects continuity.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-PREVIOUS-SCAN
    5.1. Newest-to-Oldest Previous Brief Scan
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-PREVIOUS-SCAN
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-SKIP-BLACK-RECORD Skip Black Source Record
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-SKIP-EMPTY Skip Empty Previous Brief
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-BLACK-CONTRIBUTOR Reject Brief with Black Contributor
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CONTRIBUTOR-FALLBACK Contributor Fallback
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-NO-PREVIOUS No Previous Clean Brief
    STATEMENT: The builder shall scan previous records from newest to oldest and shall select the first eligible non-empty ActiveBrief.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-SKIP-BLACK-RECORD
    5.2. Skip Black Source Record
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-SKIP-BLACK-RECORD
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-PREVIOUS-SCAN Newest-to-Oldest Previous Brief Scan
    STATEMENT: A previous record whose current tag is Black shall not supply the previous clean ActiveBrief to the builder.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-SKIP-EMPTY
    5.3. Skip Empty Previous Brief
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-SKIP-EMPTY
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-PREVIOUS-SCAN Newest-to-Oldest Previous Brief Scan
    STATEMENT: A previous record with an empty active_retrieval_brief shall not be selected as the previous clean brief.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-BLACK-CONTRIBUTOR
    5.4. Reject Brief with Black Contributor
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-BLACK-CONTRIBUTOR
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-PREVIOUS-SCAN Newest-to-Oldest Previous Brief Scan
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-BLACK AC: Black Contributor Exclusion
    STATEMENT: The builder shall reject a previous ActiveBrief when any listed contributor ID belongs to a previous record currently tagged Black.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CONTRIBUTOR-FALLBACK
    5.5. Contributor Fallback
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CONTRIBUTOR-FALLBACK
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-PREVIOUS-SCAN Newest-to-Oldest Previous Brief Scan
    STATEMENT: When an otherwise eligible previous brief has no contributor IDs, the builder shall use the selected previous record ID as its contributor list.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-NO-PREVIOUS
    5.6. No Previous Clean Brief
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-NO-PREVIOUS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-PREVIOUS-SCAN Newest-to-Oldest Previous Brief Scan
    STATEMENT: When no eligible previous clean ActiveBrief exists, the builder shall route the new record through initial ActiveBrief creation.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CURRENT-METADATA
    5.7. Current Record Metadata Payload
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CURRENT-METADATA
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    STATEMENT: The ActiveBrief agent metadata payload shall include record_id, parent_id, creation time, source, tag, retrieval source mode, active project, automatic keywords, user keywords, and embedded-file snapshot from the current MemoryRecord.

    6. 6. ActiveBrief Relevance Gate

    STATEMENT: The non-LLM gate determines whether to update the current ActiveBrief, confirm a topic shift, or propagate the existing brief without an LLM call.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT
    6.1. ActiveBrief Relevance Gate
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-QA-VECTORS Reused Q/A Vectors
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-BRIEF-CENTER ActiveBrief Center
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-NO-PREVIOUS No-Previous Pass Route
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-NO-QA No-Q/A-Vector Skip Route
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-SAFE-PASS Missing ActiveBrief Center Safe Pass
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-COSINE-SCORES Q/A-to-Center Scores
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-SMALL Top-Score Selection for Four or Fewer
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-MEDIUM Top-Score Selection for Five through Twenty
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-LARGE Top-Score Selection Above Twenty
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ACTIVE-PASS ActiveBrief Update Threshold
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-PENDING-CENTER Pending Topic Center
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOPIC-SHIFT Pending Topic Shift Threshold
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-SKIP Skip and Update Pending Topic Route
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-DIAGNOSTICS Gate Diagnostics
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-NO-LLM Gate Is Non-LLM
    STATEMENT: When enabled and a previous clean ActiveBrief exists, MemoryActiveBriefRelevanceGate shall evaluate reduced current Q/A vectors against the previous ActiveBrief and optional pending-topic center before an ActiveBrief update agent is called.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-QA-VECTORS
    6.2. Reused Q/A Vectors
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-QA-VECTORS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT ActiveBrief Relevance Gate
    STATEMENT: The gate shall reuse question_vectors and answer_vectors produced by MemorySentenceReducer and shall not re-embed the current Q/A solely for gating.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-BRIEF-CENTER
    6.3. ActiveBrief Center
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-BRIEF-CENTER
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT ActiveBrief Relevance Gate
    STATEMENT: The gate shall split the previous ActiveBrief into sentence windows, embed those windows, and calculate their compatible-vector centroid as the ActiveBrief comparison center.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-NO-PREVIOUS
    6.4. No-Previous Pass Route
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-NO-PREVIOUS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT ActiveBrief Relevance Gate
    STATEMENT: When no previous ActiveBrief is supplied, the gate shall return a pass decision with route init_no_previous_activebrief.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-NO-QA
    6.5. No-Q/A-Vector Skip Route
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-NO-QA
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT ActiveBrief Relevance Gate
    STATEMENT: When a previous brief exists but current Q/A vectors are empty, the gate shall return should_update_activebrief false with route skip_no_qa_vectors.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-SAFE-PASS
    6.6. Missing ActiveBrief Center Safe Pass
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-SAFE-PASS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT ActiveBrief Relevance Gate
    STATEMENT: When the gate cannot construct an ActiveBrief center, it shall return a safe pass with route activebrief_safe_pass rather than block the update.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-COSINE-SCORES
    6.7. Q/A-to-Center Scores
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-COSINE-SCORES
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT ActiveBrief Relevance Gate
    STATEMENT: The gate shall calculate cosine similarity between each current question/answer vector and the active comparison center.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-SMALL
    6.8. Top-Score Selection for Four or Fewer
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-SMALL
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT ActiveBrief Relevance Gate
    STATEMENT: For one through four Q/A scores, the gate shall use all scores in its top-score mean.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-MEDIUM
    6.9. Top-Score Selection for Five through Twenty
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-MEDIUM
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT ActiveBrief Relevance Gate
    STATEMENT: For five through twenty Q/A scores, the gate shall use the top four scores in its top-score mean.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-LARGE
    6.10. Top-Score Selection Above Twenty
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-LARGE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT ActiveBrief Relevance Gate
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-TOPCOUNT AC: Gate Top-Score Counts
    STATEMENT: For more than twenty Q/A scores, the gate shall use floor(n divided by five), with at least one score, in its top-score mean.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ACTIVE-PASS
    6.11. ActiveBrief Update Threshold
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ACTIVE-PASS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT ActiveBrief Relevance Gate
    STATEMENT: When the ActiveBrief comparison top-score mean is greater than or equal to activebrief_threshold, the gate shall route activebrief_update.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-PENDING-CENTER
    6.12. Pending Topic Center
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-PENDING-CENTER
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT ActiveBrief Relevance Gate
    STATEMENT: If the current Q/A fails the ActiveBrief threshold and a pending center exists, the gate shall evaluate current Q/A vectors against that pending-topic center using the same top-score rule.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOPIC-SHIFT
    6.13. Pending Topic Shift Threshold
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOPIC-SHIFT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT ActiveBrief Relevance Gate
    STATEMENT: When the pending-topic comparison top-score mean is greater than or equal to pending_topic_threshold, the gate shall route pending_topic_shift.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-SKIP
    6.14. Skip and Update Pending Topic Route
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-SKIP
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT ActiveBrief Relevance Gate
    STATEMENT: When current Q/A fails both applicable thresholds, the gate shall route skip_and_update_pending_topic and shall not authorize the normal ActiveBrief update call.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-DIAGNOSTICS
    6.15. Gate Diagnostics
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-DIAGNOSTICS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT ActiveBrief Relevance Gate
    STATEMENT: Gate diagnostics shall preserve route, reason, threshold values, score counts, selected top scores, and score summaries without requiring full vector logging.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-NO-LLM
    6.16. Gate Is Non-LLM
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-NO-LLM
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT ActiveBrief Relevance Gate
    STATEMENT: The relevance gate shall make its route deterministically from embeddings, thresholds, and pending state and shall not call an LLM.

    7. 7. ActiveBrief Init, Update, Skip, and Topic-Shift Routing

    STATEMENT: MemoryActiveRetrievalBriefBuilder maps previous-state and gate results to one of the current recording-time routes.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT
    7.1. Initial Brief Route
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT-CONTRIBUTOR Initial Contributor
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT-BUFFER Initial Route Clears Pending Buffer
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-INIT AC: Initial ActiveBrief
    STATEMENT: If no previous clean ActiveBrief exists, the builder shall invoke the configured init agent using reduced current question, reduced current answer, current record metadata, and required-output text.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT-CONTRIBUTOR
    7.2. Initial Contributor
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT-CONTRIBUTOR
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT Initial Brief Route
    STATEMENT: The initial ActiveBrief contributor list shall contain the current record_id.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT-BUFFER
    7.3. Initial Route Clears Pending Buffer
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT-BUFFER
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT Initial Brief Route
    STATEMENT: The initial ActiveBrief route shall return an empty pending-topic buffer.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-GATE-DISABLED
    7.4. Gate Disabled Route
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-GATE-DISABLED
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    STATEMENT: When a previous clean brief exists and the relevance gate is disabled, the builder shall invoke the update agent using the previous brief and current reduced Q/A and shall report route gate_disabled.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-NORMAL-UPDATE
    7.5. Related ActiveBrief Update Route
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-NORMAL-UPDATE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-RELATED AC: Related ActiveBrief Update
    STATEMENT: For gate route activebrief_update, the builder shall invoke the update agent with the previous brief and current reduced Q/A.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SAFE-PASS
    7.6. Safe-Pass Update Route
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SAFE-PASS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    STATEMENT: A gate safe-pass result authorizing update shall follow the builder update path and shall not be treated as a skipped Q/A.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-UPDATE-CONTRIBUTORS
    7.7. Update Contributor Merge
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-UPDATE-CONTRIBUTORS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    STATEMENT: For a normal or gate-disabled update, contributor IDs shall be the ordered de-duplicated merge of prior contributor IDs and the current record_id.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-UPDATE-BUFFER
    7.8. Update Clears Pending Buffer
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-UPDATE-BUFFER
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    STATEMENT: A successful normal, safe-pass, or gate-disabled update route shall return an empty pending-topic buffer.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-SHIFT
    7.9. Confirmed Topic-Shift Route
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-SHIFT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-LABELS Topic-Shift Payload Labels
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-CONTRIBUTORS Topic-Shift Contributor Merge
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-BUFFER Topic Shift Clears Pending Buffer
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-TOPIC AC: Confirmed Topic Shift
    STATEMENT: For gate route pending_topic_shift, the builder shall combine pending reduced Q/A and current reduced Q/A and shall invoke the configured update agent with the previous ActiveBrief plus that combined reduced content.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-LABELS
    7.10. Topic-Shift Payload Labels
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-LABELS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-SHIFT Confirmed Topic-Shift Route
    STATEMENT: The combined topic-shift payload shall distinguish pending question, current question, pending answer, and current answer with the current explicit labels.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-CONTRIBUTORS
    7.11. Topic-Shift Contributor Merge
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-CONTRIBUTORS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-SHIFT Confirmed Topic-Shift Route
    STATEMENT: The topic-shift contributor list shall be the ordered de-duplicated merge of prior contributors, the pending record_id, and the current record_id.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-BUFFER
    7.12. Topic Shift Clears Pending Buffer
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-BUFFER
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-SHIFT Confirmed Topic-Shift Route
    STATEMENT: A confirmed topic-shift route shall return an empty pending-topic buffer after the agent call.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SKIP
    7.13. Unrelated Q/A Skip Route
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SKIP
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SKIP-LLM-FLAG Skipped LLM Diagnostic
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-SKIP AC: Unrelated Q/A Skip
    STATEMENT: For a non-update gate route, the builder shall skip the ActiveBrief LLM call, copy the previous ActiveBrief title/body/contributor IDs into the new record result, and create a new pending-topic buffer from the current reduced Q/A.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SKIP-LLM-FLAG
    7.14. Skipped LLM Diagnostic
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SKIP-LLM-FLAG
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SKIP Unrelated Q/A Skip Route
    STATEMENT: The skip route shall report activebrief_llm_skipped true and shall preserve the gate route in activebrief_gate_route.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-NO-FABRICATION
    7.15. No Fabricated ActiveBrief Fallback
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-NO-FABRICATION
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    STATEMENT: If an init or update agent invocation or parse fails, the builder shall propagate the failure to MemoryManager and shall not invent an ActiveBrief result.

    8. 8. Pending Topic Buffer and Contributor Traceability

    STATEMENT: The pending-topic buffer supports detection of a stable new topic across consecutive skipped Q/A pairs and is not durable memory truth.
    GHOST-MEM-PENDING-RAM
    8.1. RAM-Only Pending Topic State
    UID: GHOST-MEM-PENDING-RAM
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CONTENT Pending Buffer Content
    • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CENTER Pending Center Calculation
    • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-REPLACEMENT Pending Buffer Replacement
    • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CLEAR-NEW Clear on New History
    • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CLEAR-LOAD Clear on History Load
    • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CLEAR-IMPORT Clear on Imported History
    • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-NOT-BLACKBOARD Pending Buffer Is Not Blackboard
    STATEMENT: MemoryManager.pending_activebrief_topic_buffer shall remain RAM-only and shall not be written to .ragmem, .ragmeta.json, SQLite, or the memory vector store.
    GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CONTENT
    8.2. Pending Buffer Content
    UID: GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CONTENT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-RAM RAM-Only Pending Topic State
    STATEMENT: The pending buffer shall contain current record_id, creation time, reduced question, reduced answer, question vectors, answer vectors, centroid center_vector, and vector_count.
    GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CENTER
    8.3. Pending Center Calculation
    UID: GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CENTER
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-RAM RAM-Only Pending Topic State
    STATEMENT: The pending center_vector shall be the centroid of compatible current reduced question and answer vectors.
    GHOST-MEM-PENDING-REPLACEMENT
    8.4. Pending Buffer Replacement
    UID: GHOST-MEM-PENDING-REPLACEMENT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-RAM RAM-Only Pending Topic State
    STATEMENT: Each skip route shall replace the prior pending-topic buffer with the current skipped Q/A buffer returned by the builder.
    GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CLEAR-NEW
    8.5. Clear on New History
    UID: GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CLEAR-NEW
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-RAM RAM-Only Pending Topic State
    STATEMENT: Starting a new memory history shall reset pending_activebrief_topic_buffer to an empty dictionary.
    GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CLEAR-LOAD
    8.6. Clear on History Load
    UID: GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CLEAR-LOAD
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-RAM RAM-Only Pending Topic State
    STATEMENT: Loading a memory history shall reset pending_activebrief_topic_buffer and shall not reconstruct it from persisted files.
    GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CLEAR-IMPORT
    8.7. Clear on Imported History
    UID: GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CLEAR-IMPORT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-RAM RAM-Only Pending Topic State
    STATEMENT: Persisting an imported ChatGPT history shall set pending_activebrief_topic_buffer to an empty dictionary.
    GHOST-MEM-PENDING-NOT-BLACKBOARD
    8.8. Pending Buffer Is Not Blackboard
    UID: GHOST-MEM-PENDING-NOT-BLACKBOARD
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-RAM RAM-Only Pending Topic State
    STATEMENT: The pending-topic buffer shall not be described as a Blackboard because it is a single-purpose, active-history, RAM-only continuity aid without shared task coordination or multi-component posting semantics.
    GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-STABLE
    8.9. Contributor IDs as Stable Brief Trace
    UID: GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-STABLE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-UNIQUE Contributor De-Duplication
    • GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-NO-SEMANTIC-PROOF Contributor Meaning Boundary
    STATEMENT: ActiveBrief contributor IDs shall identify the MemoryRecords whose accepted content contributed to the current stable brief.
    GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-UNIQUE
    8.10. Contributor De-Duplication
    UID: GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-UNIQUE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-STABLE Contributor IDs as Stable Brief Trace
    STATEMENT: Contributor merge shall preserve first occurrence order and shall omit blank and duplicate record IDs.
    GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-NO-SEMANTIC-PROOF
    8.11. Contributor Meaning Boundary
    UID: GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-NO-SEMANTIC-PROOF
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-STABLE Contributor IDs as Stable Brief Trace
    STATEMENT: Contributor membership shall record builder traceability and shall not by itself assert sentence-level provenance for every phrase in an LLM-produced ActiveBrief.

    9. 9. ActiveBrief Agent Definitions and Output Contract

    STATEMENT: The current init and update agents are versioned JSON synthesizers with distinct inputs and the same two-field output schema.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-ID
    9.1. ActiveBrief Agent Identity
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-ID
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-INIT-BINDINGS Init Agent Bindings
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-UPDATE-BINDINGS Update Agent Bindings
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-OUTPUT ActiveBrief Output Schema
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-NO-QA-SUMMARY No Separate Q/A Summary
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-NO-ANSWER No User-Facing Answer
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-FACT-BOUNDARY Provided-Evidence Boundary
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-CORRECTIONS Correction and Rejection Preservation
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-EMOTION Emotional Wording Boundary
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-TITLE-RULE Operational Title Rule
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-MODEL Current ActiveBrief Model Configuration
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-CALL ActiveBrief LLM Invocation
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-LIMIT-CONFLICT Current ActiveBrief Limit Inconsistency
    STATEMENT: The current ActiveBrief builder shall resolve agent_id memory_activebrief_qa_summarizer with distinct configured init and update version identifiers.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-INIT-BINDINGS
    9.2. Init Agent Bindings
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-INIT-BINDINGS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-ID ActiveBrief Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: The init agent shall bind reduced_question, reduced_answer, memory_record_metadata, and required_output and shall not require a previous ActiveBrief.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-UPDATE-BINDINGS
    9.3. Update Agent Bindings
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-UPDATE-BINDINGS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-ID ActiveBrief Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: The update agent shall bind previous_active_retrieval_brief, reduced_question, reduced_answer, memory_record_metadata, and required_output.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-OUTPUT
    9.4. ActiveBrief Output Schema
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-OUTPUT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-ID ActiveBrief Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: Both ActiveBrief agent definitions shall return JSON containing only active_retrieval_brief_title and active_retrieval_brief as declared output fields.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-NO-QA-SUMMARY
    9.5. No Separate Q/A Summary
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-NO-QA-SUMMARY
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-ID ActiveBrief Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: The ActiveBrief agent contract shall not create a separate qa_summary field.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-NO-ANSWER
    9.6. No User-Facing Answer
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-NO-ANSWER
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-ID ActiveBrief Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: The ActiveBrief agents shall not answer the original request, speak to the user, or produce final answer text.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-FACT-BOUNDARY
    9.7. Provided-Evidence Boundary
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-FACT-BOUNDARY
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-ID ActiveBrief Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: The ActiveBrief agents shall use only the supplied reduced Q/A, current metadata, and, for update, previous clean ActiveBrief and shall not import outside knowledge.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-CORRECTIONS
    9.8. Correction and Rejection Preservation
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-CORRECTIONS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-ID ActiveBrief Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: The agent instructions shall treat explicit corrections as high-importance constraints and explicit rejected interpretations as negative constraints when present in supplied evidence.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-EMOTION
    9.9. Emotional Wording Boundary
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-EMOTION
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-ID ActiveBrief Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: The agent instructions shall treat insults, frustration, short rejection, or emotional wording as feedback signals rather than automatically converting them into new topic content.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-TITLE-RULE
    9.10. Operational Title Rule
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-TITLE-RULE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-ID ActiveBrief Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: The current agent definitions shall request a neutral factual operational title of ten to twenty words describing the stable working-conversation center.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-MODEL
    9.11. Current ActiveBrief Model Configuration
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-MODEL
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-ID ActiveBrief Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: The current init and update JSON definitions shall configure provider openai, model gpt-5-mini, temperature zero, JSON response format, and max_tokens 600.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-CALL
    9.12. ActiveBrief LLM Invocation
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-CALL
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-ID ActiveBrief Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: The builder shall compose the selected AgentPrompt, call LLMClient.responses with the agent model and maximum output tokens, minimal reasoning effort, metadata return enabled, and the configured prompt cache key, then parse through AgentPrompt.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-LIMIT-CONFLICT
    9.13. Current ActiveBrief Limit Inconsistency
    UID: GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-LIMIT-CONFLICT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-ID ActiveBrief Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: The current baseline shall record that runtime_config target_brief_tokens and builder required-output text use about 700 tokens while the current init/update agent hard rules specify an absolute maximum of 500 tokens.

    The implementation and requirements shall not claim one unified effective limit until this inconsistency is resolved in the versioned configuration and agent definitions.

    10. 10. Runtime Memory Compression

    STATEMENT: MemoryCompressor performs query-dependent reduction of selected episodic Q/A candidates before Memory Context synthesis.
    GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT
    10.1. Runtime Memory Compressor
    UID: GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-CONFIG Compression Configuration
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-CURRENT-SCOPE Current Episodic-Only Compression
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-DISABLED Compression Disabled Passthrough
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-CLEAN Effective Query Normalization
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-BELOW Below-Budget Query Preservation
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-ORDER Query Sentence Order
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-SKIP Oversized Query Sentence Skip
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-FALLBACK Query Fallback Slice
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-EMBED-ONCE Single Query Anchor Embedding
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-NO-ANCHOR Missing Query Anchor Passthrough
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-PRESERVE-ORIGINAL Original Episodic Text Preservation
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-MODE Compression Mode Metadata
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-NO-STORE-WRITE No Compression Persistence
    STATEMENT: MemoryCompressor shall receive runtime configuration and shall produce query-dependent compressed fields without modifying durable MemoryRecord truth.
    GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-CONFIG
    10.2. Compression Configuration
    UID: GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-CONFIG
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT Runtime Memory Compressor
    STATEMENT: MemoryCompressor shall consume the memory_compression section, including global enabled, effective-query maximum tokens and embedding model, and episodic reduction enablement, total budget, question budget, windowing, and redundancy threshold.
    GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-CURRENT-SCOPE
    10.3. Current Episodic-Only Compression
    UID: GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-CURRENT-SCOPE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT Runtime Memory Compressor
    STATEMENT: The current compressor shall operate on selected episodic candidates only and shall not compress working-memory candidates, Direct Recall candidates, or semantic memory chunks in the current retrieval flow.
    GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-DISABLED
    10.4. Compression Disabled Passthrough
    UID: GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-DISABLED
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT Runtime Memory Compressor
    STATEMENT: When global or episodic compression is disabled, compress_episodic_candidates shall return the candidate list without adding compressed fields.
    GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-CLEAN
    10.5. Effective Query Normalization
    UID: GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-CLEAN
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT Runtime Memory Compressor
    STATEMENT: The compressor shall strip the effective retrieval query before reducing or embedding it.
    GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-BELOW
    10.6. Below-Budget Query Preservation
    UID: GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-BELOW
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT Runtime Memory Compressor
    STATEMENT: When the effective query is within query_max_tokens, query reduction shall preserve it unchanged.
    GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-ORDER
    10.7. Query Sentence Order
    UID: GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-ORDER
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT Runtime Memory Compressor
    STATEMENT: When the effective query exceeds its budget, deterministic query reduction shall inspect sentences in original order and append each sentence that fits the remaining budget.
    GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-SKIP
    10.8. Oversized Query Sentence Skip
    UID: GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-SKIP
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT Runtime Memory Compressor
    STATEMENT: A query sentence that would exceed the configured query budget shall be skipped rather than partially included in the normal sentence-selection path.
    GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-FALLBACK
    10.9. Query Fallback Slice
    UID: GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-FALLBACK
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT Runtime Memory Compressor
    STATEMENT: If no complete sentence fits, query reduction shall use the current bounded first-sentence character slice without generating new content.
    GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-EMBED-ONCE
    10.10. Single Query Anchor Embedding
    UID: GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-EMBED-ONCE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT Runtime Memory Compressor
    STATEMENT: After deterministic query reduction, the compressor shall embed the reduced query once and shall use that vector as the external anchor for all selected episodic candidates in the run.
    GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-NO-ANCHOR
    10.11. Missing Query Anchor Passthrough
    UID: GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-NO-ANCHOR
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT Runtime Memory Compressor
    STATEMENT: When no query anchor vector can be produced, the compressor shall return episodic candidates unchanged rather than fabricate compressed evidence.
    GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-PRESERVE-ORIGINAL
    10.12. Original Episodic Text Preservation
    UID: GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-PRESERVE-ORIGINAL
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT Runtime Memory Compressor
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-COMPRESS AC: Query-Anchored Episodic Compression
    STATEMENT: Compressed candidates shall retain original input_text and output_text and shall add compressed_input_text and compressed_output_text as runtime fields.
    GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-MODE
    10.13. Compression Mode Metadata
    UID: GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-MODE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT Runtime Memory Compressor
    STATEMENT: Each compressed episodic candidate shall identify memory_compression_mode as query_anchor_episode and shall include query and reducer diagnostics.
    GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-NO-STORE-WRITE
    10.14. No Compression Persistence
    UID: GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-NO-STORE-WRITE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT Runtime Memory Compressor
    STATEMENT: MemoryCompressor shall not update .ragmem, .ragmeta.json, SQLite, or memory vectors with query-dependent compressed text.

    11. 11. MemoryMergeSynthesizer Inputs and Evidence Construction

    STATEMENT: MemoryMergeSynthesizer receives bounded runtime evidence and constructs one explicit agent payload.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT
    11.1. Memory Context Synthesizer
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CONFIG Synthesis Configuration
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EXPLICIT-CONFIG Current Runtime Configuration Injection
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-DISABLED Disabled Synthesis
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EPISODIC-CAP Episodic Input Cap
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-SEMANTIC-CAP Semantic Chunk Input Cap
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CURRENT-EVIDENCE-CLASSES Current Synthesized Evidence Classes
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EPISODIC-FORMAT Episodic Evidence Format
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-SEMANTIC-FORMAT Semantic Evidence Format
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-EVIDENCE No Evidence Skip
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-PAYLOAD Synthesis Payload
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ACTIVEBRIEF-REFERENCE ActiveBrief Reference Boundary
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall synthesize one query-relevant memory_context string from current episodic candidates and semantic memory chunks using the effective retrieval query and ActiveRetrievalBrief as an orientation and anti-duplication reference.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CONFIG
    11.2. Synthesis Configuration
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CONFIG
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT Memory Context Synthesizer
    STATEMENT: The synthesizer shall consume memory_merge_synthesizer enabled, agent_id, agent_version, target_context_tokens, max_episodic_records, max_semantic_chunks, and prompt_cache_key.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EXPLICIT-CONFIG
    11.3. Current Runtime Configuration Injection
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EXPLICIT-CONFIG
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT Memory Context Synthesizer
    STATEMENT: The current MemoryRetriever shall construct MemoryMergeSynthesizer with the active runtime_config dictionary so the normal retrieval path does not depend on the synthesizer fallback configuration loader.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-DISABLED
    11.4. Disabled Synthesis
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-DISABLED
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT Memory Context Synthesizer
    STATEMENT: When synthesis is disabled, the synthesizer shall skip the LLM and return empty memory_context with a disabled diagnostic.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EPISODIC-CAP
    11.5. Episodic Input Cap
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EPISODIC-CAP
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT Memory Context Synthesizer
    STATEMENT: Before evidence formatting, the synthesizer shall retain at most the first max_episodic_records candidates in their existing order.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-SEMANTIC-CAP
    11.6. Semantic Chunk Input Cap
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-SEMANTIC-CAP
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT Memory Context Synthesizer
    STATEMENT: Before evidence formatting, the synthesizer shall retain at most the first max_semantic_chunks in their existing order.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CURRENT-EVIDENCE-CLASSES
    11.7. Current Synthesized Evidence Classes
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CURRENT-EVIDENCE-CLASSES
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT Memory Context Synthesizer
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-WORKING Working Memory Not Synthesizer Input
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-DIRECT Direct Recall Not Synthesizer Input
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-SYNTH-INPUT AC: Current Synthesis Evidence Classes
    STATEMENT: The current synthesizer evidence source shall include reduced episodic episodes and semantic memory chunks.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-WORKING
    11.8. Working Memory Not Synthesizer Input
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-WORKING
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CURRENT-EVIDENCE-CLASSES Current Synthesized Evidence Classes
    STATEMENT: The current synthesizer shall not receive MemoryContextPack.working_memory_candidates as memory_evidence.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-DIRECT
    11.9. Direct Recall Not Synthesizer Input
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-DIRECT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CURRENT-EVIDENCE-CLASSES Current Synthesized Evidence Classes
    STATEMENT: The current synthesizer shall not receive MemoryContextPack.direct_recall_candidate as memory_evidence.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EPISODIC-FORMAT
    11.10. Episodic Evidence Format
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EPISODIC-FORMAT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT Memory Context Synthesizer
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-COMPRESSED-FIRST Compressed Episodic Text Priority
    STATEMENT: For each episodic candidate, evidence text shall identify episode index, record_id, optional tag/source/score, and available QUESTION and ANSWER blocks.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-COMPRESSED-FIRST
    11.11. Compressed Episodic Text Priority
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-COMPRESSED-FIRST
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EPISODIC-FORMAT Episodic Evidence Format
    STATEMENT: Episodic evidence formatting shall use compressed_input_text and compressed_output_text when present and shall otherwise fall back to original input_text and output_text.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-SEMANTIC-FORMAT
    11.12. Semantic Evidence Format
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-SEMANTIC-FORMAT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT Memory Context Synthesizer
    STATEMENT: For each semantic chunk, evidence text shall identify available vector_id, record_id, role, score, and TEXT content.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-EVIDENCE
    11.13. No Evidence Skip
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-EVIDENCE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT Memory Context Synthesizer
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-NO-EVIDENCE AC: Empty Evidence
    STATEMENT: When the formatted episodic and semantic evidence text is empty, the synthesizer shall skip the LLM and return empty memory_context with reason no_memory_evidence.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-PAYLOAD
    11.14. Synthesis Payload
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-PAYLOAD
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT Memory Context Synthesizer
    STATEMENT: The synthesis payload shall contain effective_retrieval_query_text, active_retrieval_brief_title, active_retrieval_brief, memory_evidence, and required_output.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ACTIVEBRIEF-REFERENCE
    11.15. ActiveBrief Reference Boundary
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ACTIVEBRIEF-REFERENCE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT Memory Context Synthesizer
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ANTI-DUP-NATURE Anti-Duplication Instruction Nature
    STATEMENT: ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be passed as orientation and anti-duplication reference and shall not be treated as a substitute for missing episodic or semantic evidence.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ANTI-DUP-NATURE
    11.16. Anti-Duplication Instruction Nature
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ANTI-DUP-NATURE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ACTIVEBRIEF-REFERENCE ActiveBrief Reference Boundary
    STATEMENT: ActiveBrief anti-duplication is currently an instruction to the synthesizer agent and shall not be represented as a deterministic overlap detector.

    12. 12. Memory Synthesizer Agent Contract and Output

    STATEMENT: The current memory_synthesizer_001 agent produces one structured backend field and is constrained to provided evidence.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID
    12.1. Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT Memory Context Synthesizer
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-TYPE Memory Synthesizer Agent Type
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-MODEL Current Memory Synthesizer Model
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-BINDINGS Memory Synthesizer Bindings
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-OUTPUT Memory Context Output Schema
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-QUERY-RELEVANCE Query-Relevant Context
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-PRESERVE-FACTS Concrete Memory Fact Preservation
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-DEDUPE Evidence De-Duplication Instruction
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-INVENTION No Invented Memory
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-ANSWER Backend Context Only
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-CALL Memory Synthesis Invocation
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-SUCCESS Successful Synthesis Result
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-DIAGNOSTICS Successful Synthesis Diagnostics
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-FAILURE Synthesis Failure Result
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-FABRICATION No Fabricated Context Fallback
    STATEMENT: The current synthesizer shall resolve agent_id memory_synthesizer and agent version memory_synthesizer_001.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-TYPE
    12.2. Memory Synthesizer Agent Type
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-TYPE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: The current agent definition shall identify the agent as a Synthesizer named Memory Context Synthesizer.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-MODEL
    12.3. Current Memory Synthesizer Model
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-MODEL
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: The current agent definition shall configure provider openai, model gpt-5-mini, temperature zero, max_tokens 1200, and JSON response format.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-BINDINGS
    12.4. Memory Synthesizer Bindings
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-BINDINGS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: The agent definition shall bind effective retrieval query, optional ActiveBrief title, optional ActiveBrief body, memory evidence, and required-output instructions.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-OUTPUT
    12.5. Memory Context Output Schema
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-OUTPUT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: The agent output schema shall declare one result field named memory_context and shall not declare a final answer field.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-QUERY-RELEVANCE
    12.6. Query-Relevant Context
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-QUERY-RELEVANCE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: The agent instructions shall retain only memory evidence connected to the effective retrieval query and shall return an empty memory_context when no useful evidence exists.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-PRESERVE-FACTS
    12.7. Concrete Memory Fact Preservation
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-PRESERVE-FACTS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: When relevant, the agent instructions shall preserve concrete names, technical entities, decisions, constraints, corrections, and rejected interpretations from supplied memory evidence.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-DEDUPE
    12.8. Evidence De-Duplication Instruction
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-DEDUPE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: The agent instructions shall combine duplicate episodic and semantic evidence instead of repeating the same point.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-INVENTION
    12.9. No Invented Memory
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-INVENTION
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: The agent shall not invent facts, preferences, project decisions, files, or implementation status and shall not import outside knowledge.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-ANSWER
    12.10. Backend Context Only
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-ANSWER
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: The agent shall not answer the original request, address the user, or produce a final user-facing response.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-CALL
    12.11. Memory Synthesis Invocation
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-CALL
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall compose the configured AgentPrompt, call LLMClient.responses with minimal reasoning effort, metadata return enabled, and the configured prompt-cache key, and shall parse the returned content through AgentPrompt.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-SUCCESS
    12.12. Successful Synthesis Result
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-SUCCESS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: On successful parse, the synthesizer shall return stripped memory_context, synthesis diagnostics, and memory_synthesis_llm_skipped false.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-DIAGNOSTICS
    12.13. Successful Synthesis Diagnostics
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-DIAGNOSTICS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: Successful synthesis diagnostics shall include reason synthesized, configured target, selected input counts, output character count, provider usage, model name, status, and incomplete reason when available.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-FAILURE
    12.14. Synthesis Failure Result
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-FAILURE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-FAILURE AC: Synthesis Failure
    STATEMENT: If composition, provider call, or parse raises an exception, the synthesizer shall return empty memory_context, a synthesis_failed diagnostic containing the error and input counts, and memory_synthesis_llm_skipped true.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-FABRICATION
    12.15. No Fabricated Context Fallback
    UID: GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-FABRICATION
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity
    STATEMENT: A failed or skipped synthesis shall not be replaced with an invented deterministic Memory Context.

    13. 13. MemoryContextPack and SuperPrompt Handoff

    STATEMENT: MemoryRetriever stores raw candidate classes and synthesis output separately and projects only the synthesized context into the normal prompt view.
    GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-PACK
    13.1. Pack Synthesis Assignment
    UID: GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-PACK
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    STATEMENT: After synthesis, MemoryRetriever shall store the returned memory_context and synthesis diagnostics through MemoryContextPack.set_synthesized_memory_context.
    GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SP-TEXT
    13.2. SuperPrompt Memory Context Text
    UID: GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SP-TEXT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-HANDOFF AC: SuperPrompt Memory Handoff
    STATEMENT: MemoryRetriever shall assign MemoryContextPack.synthesized_memory_context to SuperPrompt.memory_context_text.
    GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SP-PACK
    13.3. SuperPrompt Raw Memory Pack
    UID: GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SP-PACK
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    STATEMENT: MemoryRetriever shall retain the full MemoryContextPack object and its dictionary/debug representations separately from memory_context_text.
    GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-ACTIVEBRIEF
    13.4. SuperPrompt ActiveBrief Snapshot
    UID: GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-ACTIVEBRIEF
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    STATEMENT: MemoryRetriever shall store the latest current eligible ActiveBrief title and body in the dedicated SuperPrompt fields and extras independently of synthesized Memory Context.
    GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-RENDER
    13.5. Normal Memory Context Rendering
    UID: GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-RENDER
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SUPPORTING Supporting Context Semantics
    STATEMENT: SuperPromptProjector normal rendering shall use memory_context_text under the Memory Context section and shall not render raw MemoryContextPack debug Markdown by default.
    GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SUPPORTING
    13.6. Supporting Context Semantics
    UID: GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SUPPORTING
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-RENDER Normal Memory Context Rendering
